tasks.spec:
You are a Ringer worker editing ONE existing file in a git worktree of the ringer repo. Your current working directory IS that worktree — edit files here directly, leave ALL changes uncommitted (never run git commit; the check exports your diff as a patch), and never touch anything outside this directory. You own EXACTLY ONE file: ringer.py (large, ~9100 lines — make a small, surgical change; the final diff should be a few dozen lines).

GOAL: ringer's HTML report pages currently show only the manifest's model string (e.g. feeder/auto/coding). A separate tool now writes real routing telemetry into the run state as an optional per-task dict task["feeder"], shaped like: {"sessions": ["ses_x"], "served": [{"platform": "sambanova", "model_id": "DeepSeek-V3.1", "calls": 2, "output_tokens": 38}, {"platform": "nvidia", "model_id": "mistral-large-3", "calls": 1, "output_tokens": 2}], "failovers": 1, "mixed_models": true, "requests": 4, "errors_429": 1, "latency_ms_total": 2270, "latency_ms_p50": 610}. Render it.

IMPLEMENT: when (and only when) a task dict carries a non-empty "feeder" dict, the python-rendered report pages must show ONE compact telemetry strip for that task, of the form: Served by: sambanova/DeepSeek-V3.1 (2 calls, 38 tok) \u2192 nvidia/mistral-large-3 (1 call, 2 tok) \u00b7 1 failover \u00b7 p50 610ms — models joined with \u2192 in list order, failover count only when > 0, p50 when present. Add it in BOTH python renderers that show per-task detail: the final report page (module-level render_final_report_html and its helpers, around lines 3283-3624) and the live status page (render_status_html path). Write ONE small helper function, e.g. render_feeder_strip(task_dict) -> str returning '' when the block is absent/empty/malformed, and call it from both task-rendering paths.

TWO PREVIOUS ATTEMPTS FAILED — do not repeat their mistakes:
1. NameError at render time: the helper referenced a variable from another scope. Your helper must reference ONLY its own parameter. At each call site, FIRST read the enclosing function to learn what the task dict variable is actually called there (task, item, row — it differs per function) and pass THAT variable.
2. SyntaxError from a broken string boundary: an attempt inserted CSS text that landed OUTSIDE a triple-quoted string, turning CSS into bare python at module level. Therefore: DO NOT ADD OR EDIT ANY CSS AT ALL. Do not touch any <style> template or CSS string. Reuse existing classes on your HTML (e.g. class="work-kind" or class="mono") — an unstyled plain <div> is acceptable. Your HTML strip must be built inside a normal python f-string in the helper, using the existing html_escape() on every interpolated value.

DISCIPLINE: after EVERY single edit to ringer.py, run: python3 -m py_compile ringer.py — and fix any syntax error before doing anything else. Never re-indent or reformat existing code; only insert the helper function plus one short call-site line per renderer.

HOW TO VERIFY (this exact command is the task's check — run it yourself from the worktree root until it passes): sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/phase4_render_check.sh
The check compiles ringer.py, then renders a two-task state (alpha WITH the feeder block above, bravo WITHOUT) via ringer.render_final_report_html(state, renderer=None, page_path=None) and asserts: 'Served by' appears EXACTLY once, both model names appear, and nothing raises.

HARD RULES: only ringer.py may be changed; no git commit; no CSS or style-block edits anywhere; preserve ringer's baked-in invariants (stdin closed, explicit sandbox flags, executed verification, raw logs — do not touch worker-invocation code); keep the change minimal and localized to rendering.
tasks.check: mkdir -p /tmp/ringer-phase4-telemetry/patches && git add -A && git diff --cached > /tmp/ringer-phase4-telemetry/patches/render-hook.patch && sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/phase4_render_check.sh
tasks.verified: ringer.py compiles and the rendered report shows the Served-by telemetry strip exactly once for the task that has telemetry, with fail-open behavior proven for the task that lacks it
